# cloudflare


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

## CF

Thin wrapper around the official `cloudflare` Python SDK. Token is read
from the `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` environment variable by default.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/cfeasy/blob/main/cfeasy/core.py#L96"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### CF.setup_tunnel

``` python

def setup_tunnel(
    domain:str, name:str, tunnel_name:str | None=None, proxied:bool=True
):

```

*One-liner for VPS hosting. Creates (or reuses) a tunnel, sets up the
CNAME subdomain, and returns (tunnel_id, token) ready for
cloudflared_svc() in your docker-compose.*

Usage in fastops: tid, token = setup_tunnel(“example.com”, “app”) \# →
app.example.com \# then in Compose: \# .svc(“cloudflared”,
\*\*cloudflared_svc(token_env=…)) with CF_TUNNEL_TOKEN=token

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/cfeasy/blob/main/cfeasy/core.py#L13"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### CF

``` python

def CF(
    token:NoneType=None, timeout:int=10, kw:VAR_KEYWORD
):

```

*Cloudflare API wrapper using the official cloudflare Python SDK*

### Least-privilege token setup

Create a **Custom Token** in the Cloudflare dashboard (My Profile → API
Tokens → Create Token):

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Capability</th>
<th>Required permission</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>dns_read</code> / DNS ops</td>
<td>Zone → DNS → <strong>Edit</strong> (scope to specific zone)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>account_read</code> / tunnel ops</td>
<td>Account → Account Settings → <strong>Read</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>tunnel_read</code> / tunnel ops</td>
<td>Account → Cloudflare Tunnel → <strong>Edit</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Use `CF().verify()` to confirm your token has the permissions it needs
before running ops.

``` python
c = CF()
c.verify()
```

    {'result': True}
